mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to higher nominal interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Industrial Production (INDPRO) over 6 months indicates rising economic activity, leading to higher demand for raw materials and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in Housing Starts (HOUST) over 3 months signals rising housing demand, leading to higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-housing-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to higher nominal interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-interest-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in Real Personal Consumption Expenditures (RSAFS) over 3 months signals rising consumer spending, leading to higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-consumer-spending periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) and a 1% increase in Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) over 3 months signal a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls < 0.5% and PAYEMS rises > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment and high-wage-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
